title: Vague code review request rewrite
originalPrompt: Can you please take a look at my code and let me know if there are any issues or problems with it? I would really appreciate your help with this.
options: Review this Python function for bugs, edge cases, and performance issues. Return a bullet list of findings with line references.
Please look at my code and tell me what you think about it and if there are any problems.
Check the code.
hint: A good code review prompt specifies the language, what to look for, and the desired output format.

title: Best prompt for writing unit tests
originalPrompt: You need a prompt to ask an AI to write unit tests for a JavaScript function that validates email addresses.
options: Write unit tests for my code.
Write Jest unit tests for this JavaScript email validation function. Cover: valid emails, missing @, missing domain, empty string, and null input. Use describe/it blocks.
Can you help me write some tests? I have a function that checks emails and I want to make sure it works correctly with different inputs.
hint: Naming the testing framework, function purpose, and specific test cases eliminates ambiguity and produces ready-to-use output.

title: Debugging help prompt improvement
originalPrompt: My app is throwing an error and I'm not sure why. Can you help me figure out what's wrong and how to fix it?
options: Debug this error. App is broken. Error message attached.
I'm getting an error in my Node.js Express app. Here is the stack trace: [paste]. The error occurs when POST /api/users is called with a duplicate email. Identify the root cause and suggest a fix.
Please help me debug my application because it's not working properly and I've been stuck on this for a while and can't figure out what is happening.
hint: Include the runtime, the triggering action, and the exact error output so the AI can diagnose without guessing.

title: Compact refactoring request
originalPrompt: Ask the AI to refactor a legacy Python function to use modern syntax.
options: Refactor this Python 2 function to Python 3.10+. Use f-strings, type hints, and replace map/filter with comprehensions. Return only the refactored code.

I have some old Python code that was written a long time ago and it uses older Python 2 syntax and style. I would like you to please help me modernize it by converting it to use Python 3 conventions including things like f-strings and type hints and list comprehensions instead of map and filter calls.
Update my Python code.
hint: Specifying the target version, exact syntax features, and output format keeps the prompt under budget while being fully actionable.

title: Architecture decision best prompt
originalPrompt: Your team must decide between a monolith and microservices for a new SaaS product with 5 engineers and 10k expected users at launch.
options: What architecture should I use for my app?

Compare monolith vs microservices for a B2B SaaS product: 5-person engineering team, 10k users at launch, expected 10x growth in 18 months. Evaluate on: operational complexity, deployment speed, team scaling, and cost. Recommend one and justify in 3 bullet points.
Tell me about microservices architecture and whether it's good or bad for startups and what the tradeoffs are compared to other approaches.
hint: Grounding the prompt in concrete constraints (team size, user count, growth) and specifying evaluation criteria produces a directly actionable recommendation.

title: PR description generation prompt fix
originalPrompt: Write a PR description for my changes.
options: Write a GitHub PR description for this diff. Include: what changed and why, any breaking changes, and testing instructions. Use the conventional commits format. Diff: [paste diff].
Create a description for my pull request that explains what I did.
Write a PR description.
hint: Providing the diff and specifying the required sections and format format turns a one-liner into a complete, merge-ready PR description.

title: API documentation prompt selection
originalPrompt: You need to generate REST API documentation for a /users endpoint.
options: Document my API endpoint.
Write OpenAPI 3.0 documentation for GET /users. Include: path parameters, query filters (page, limit, role), response schema with example, and 401/404 error responses.
Can you help me write some documentation for an endpoint in my API? It's for getting users and it has some filtering options.
hint: Naming the spec format (OpenAPI 3.0) and listing every parameter and response code produces documentation that is immediately usable.

title: Concise SQL query generation request
originalPrompt: Ask AI to write a SQL query to find duplicate records.
options: Write a SQL query to find duplicate email addresses in a users table. Return email and count. Use GROUP BY and HAVING.

I need your help writing a SQL query. I have a database table called users and I want to find all the email addresses that appear more than once in the table because we have duplicate records and I need to identify them so we can clean up the data.
Find duplicates in SQL.
hint: Naming the table, the duplicate field, and the SQL clauses expected produces a runnable query without excess context.

title: Legacy code refactoring strategy prompt
originalPrompt: How do I refactor old code? I have a big codebase and it's really hard to work with and I want to make it better but I don't know where to start.
options: Suggest a prioritised refactoring plan for a 200k-line PHP 5.6 monolith being migrated to Laravel 10. Constraints: zero downtime, no dedicated refactoring sprints. Output: ordered list of 5 steps with rationale and risk level for each.
How should I refactor old code to make it better?

Give me a detailed and comprehensive explanation of all the different strategies and approaches for refactoring large legacy codebases, including the pros and cons of each approach and what situations they are best suited for.
hint: Expert prompts specify the technology stack, hard constraints, desired output structure, and scope so the AI produces an immediately executable plan rather than general advice.

title: Choosing best error handling prompt
originalPrompt: You want guidance on adding robust error handling to an async Node.js service.
options: Add error handling to my Node.js code.
What is error handling and why is it important in Node.js applications for production environments?

Add error handling to this async Node.js Express route handler. Use try/catch, return structured JSON errors ({error, message, statusCode}), log with Winston, and handle both 4xx client errors and 5xx server errors. Code: [paste].
hint: Specifying the error structure, logging library, and HTTP status code categories gives the AI everything needed to produce production-ready code.

title: Compact system design prompt
originalPrompt: Ask AI to design a rate-limiting system for an API.
options: Design a token-bucket rate limiter for a REST API: 100 req/min per user, Redis-backed, returns 429 with Retry-After header. Provide pseudocode and Redis key schema.

I need help designing a rate limiting system for my API. We want to limit how many requests users can make. Please explain the different algorithms like token bucket and sliding window and leaky bucket and tell me which one you think is best and how I would implement it using Redis.
How do I rate limit an API?
hint: Choosing the algorithm, specifying numeric limits, the backing store, and the expected output format keeps the prompt tight and the answer immediately usable.

title: Code explanation prompt improvement
originalPrompt: Can you explain what this code does? I want to understand it better.
options: Explain what this Python code does in plain English. Describe the purpose of each function, identify any side effects, and flag any potential bugs. Code: [paste].
What does this code do?

I have some code here that was written by someone else and I'm having trouble understanding it and would like a thorough explanation of what it does and how it works so that I can maintain it in the future.
hint: Asking for function-level breakdown, side effects, and bug flags turns a vague request into a structured code audit.

title: Best prompt for database indexing advice
originalPrompt: A PostgreSQL table with 50M rows is running slow queries on a composite filter.
options: My database is slow. How do I make it faster?

Analyse this slow PostgreSQL query on a 50M-row orders table. Filters: status='pending' AND created_at > NOW()-7d. EXPLAIN ANALYZE output: [paste]. Recommend indexes, and confirm whether a partial index on status would reduce index size.
Tell me about PostgreSQL indexing strategies and when to use different types of indexes like B-tree and hash and partial indexes and what the tradeoffs are.
hint: Providing the query, table size, filter columns, and EXPLAIN output lets the AI give a targeted index recommendation rather than generic advice.

title: Tight prompt for concurrency design
originalPrompt: Ask AI to help design a thread-safe cache.
options: Design a thread-safe LRU cache in Go: capacity 1000, read-heavy (95% reads), use sync.RWMutex. Show struct definition and Get/Put signatures.

Help me design a thread-safe LRU cache implementation in Go. The cache should have a configurable capacity and support concurrent read and write access. I want to understand the best locking strategy to use, whether that's a simple mutex or a read-write mutex, and you should explain the tradeoffs and show me a full implementation with all the methods.
How do I make a thread-safe cache?
hint: Specifying language, capacity, read/write ratio, and limiting output to struct + signatures produces a focused design under the token budget.

title: Improve vague regex help request
originalPrompt: I need a regex. Can you help me write one for what I need?
options: Write a JavaScript regex to validate a UK mobile phone number: starts with 07, 11 digits total, no spaces. Return the pattern and a one-line explanation.
Can you write a regular expression for me? I need it to match phone numbers.
Write a comprehensive regex that can handle all kinds of phone number formats from different countries with various separators and country codes.
hint: Defining the language, exact format rules, and asking for a brief explanation makes the regex immediately testable.

title: Choosing migration script prompt
originalPrompt: You need to generate a database migration script to add a column.
options: Write a database migration.
Write a PostgreSQL migration script to add a non-null varchar(255) column called full_name to the users table, with default '' for existing rows. Include rollback SQL.

I need to add a new column to my users table in PostgreSQL. The column should store names and shouldn't allow null values. Can you write a migration script that does this safely and also lets me roll it back if needed?
hint: Specifying the DB engine, column type, constraints, default value, and requesting rollback SQL produces a complete, reversible migration in one shot.

title: Security code review prompt fix
originalPrompt: Review my code for security issues please.
options: Review this Node.js Express endpoint for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities. Flag SQL injection, XSS, and missing auth middleware. For each finding, give severity (High/Med/Low) and a one-line fix. Code: [paste].
Look at my code and check if there are any security vulnerabilities or problems that could be exploited by hackers.
Do a security review.
hint: Naming the framework, the vulnerability taxonomy, and requiring severity + fix per finding produces an actionable security audit.

title: Best prompt for README generation
originalPrompt: You need to generate a README for a new open-source CLI tool.
options: Write a README for my project.
Generate a GitHub README for an open-source Python CLI tool called 'csv-cleaner'. Include: one-line description, installation via pip, 3 usage examples with flags, and a contributing section.

Write a detailed and comprehensive README file for my Python command-line tool that explains everything about it including how to install it and use it and contribute to it and all the different options and features.
hint: Naming the tool, its type, and listing the exact sections prevents the AI from guessing what to include.

title: Compact code smell identification
originalPrompt: Ask AI to identify code smells in a function.
options: List code smells in this function. Flag: long method, magic numbers, deep nesting, and duplicated logic. Return a numbered list. Code: [paste].
Can you look at this function I wrote and tell me if there are any code smells or anti-patterns? I want to improve the quality of my code and make it more maintainable and readable.
Check my code for problems.
hint: Pre-listing the specific smells to check and asking for a numbered list focuses the output and keeps the prompt under budget.

title: Event-driven architecture review prompt
originalPrompt: I want to use events in my system. Can you help me design this?
options: Review this event-driven architecture for an e-commerce order system using Kafka. Identify: ordering guarantees issues, consumer group design flaws, and dead-letter queue strategy. System diagram: [paste]. Return findings as a prioritised table.
Help me design an event-driven system using message queues or something similar.
Tell me everything about event-driven architecture, event sourcing, CQRS, and how Kafka works and when I should use it versus other message brokers.
hint: Providing the existing design, naming the technology, and specifying what to review (guarantees, consumer groups, DLQ) produces a targeted expert critique rather than a tutorial.

title: Vague Dockerfile help request fix
originalPrompt: Can you help me write a Dockerfile? I want to containerize my app.
options: Write a production-ready Dockerfile for a Node.js 20 Express app. Use multi-stage build, non-root user, and COPY only package.json before npm ci to leverage layer caching.
Help me write a Dockerfile for my Node.js application.
Please write a very detailed Dockerfile for me with all the best practices and optimisations and security features that I should use for a Node.js application.
hint: Specifying the runtime version, build strategy, security requirement, and a specific optimisation gives the AI a clear target.

title: Best CI/CD pipeline troubleshoot prompt
originalPrompt: Your GitHub Actions workflow is failing on the build step intermittently.
options: My CI pipeline is broken. Fix it.
My GitHub Actions build step fails intermittently (~30% of runs) with 'ECONNRESET' during npm ci. The runner is ubuntu-latest. Workflow YAML: [paste]. Suggest the most likely cause and a fix.
Tell me about common GitHub Actions errors and how to debug CI/CD pipelines in general and what are best practices for writing reliable workflows.
hint: Providing the error message, failure rate, runner OS, and the workflow YAML gives the AI what it needs to diagnose the specific issue.

title: Kubernetes config review prompt fix
originalPrompt: Can you review my Kubernetes configuration? I want to make sure it's correct.
options: Review this Kubernetes Deployment manifest for production readiness. Check: resource requests/limits, liveness/readiness probes, image tag (no 'latest'), and replica count. YAML: [paste]. Return findings as a checklist.
Please review my Kubernetes YAML configuration file and let me know if there are any issues or improvements I should make.
Check my Kubernetes config.
hint: Listing the exact checklist items (resources, probes, image tag) and the desired output format makes the review focused and directly actionable.

title: Compact runbook generation request
originalPrompt: Ask AI to write a runbook for restarting a service.
options: Write a runbook for restarting the payments-service in Kubernetes. Steps: check pod status, drain connections, restart deployment, verify health check. Format as numbered steps.

I need you to write a detailed runbook or operational procedure document for restarting one of our microservices that runs in Kubernetes. Please include all the steps an on-call engineer would need to follow.
Write a runbook.
hint: Naming the service, the platform, the key steps, and the format makes the runbook production-ready without excess words.

title: Best prompt for incident postmortem
originalPrompt: You need to draft a postmortem for a 45-minute database outage that affected checkout.
options: Write a postmortem.

Write a blameless incident postmortem for a 45-minute PostgreSQL primary failover that took down checkout. Include: timeline, root cause, customer impact (est. £12k lost revenue), and 3 action items with owners. Tone: blameless.

Write a full and detailed incident postmortem document for a database outage that we had, including a very detailed timeline and root cause analysis and everything the team did to fix it and lots of recommendations for preventing it in the future.
hint: Including the incident type, duration, business impact, and blameless tone constraint produces a postmortem that matches real-world engineering standards.

title: Terraform module review prompt fix
originalPrompt: Review my Terraform code and tell me if it's good.
options: Review this Terraform module for AWS EKS. Check: hardcoded credentials, missing remote state backend, security group overpermissioning, and resource tagging. Return a finding per line with severity. Code: [paste].
Can you review my Terraform infrastructure-as-code and let me know if there are any security issues or best practices I'm not following?
Review Terraform.
hint: Naming the cloud provider, resource type, and specific checks (credentials, state, permissions, tagging) scopes the review precisely.

title: Tight Helm chart troubleshoot prompt
originalPrompt: Ask AI to debug a failing Helm chart deployment.
options: Debug this Helm chart deployment failure on EKS. Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'. Chart values.yaml: [paste]. Check: image tag, registry auth, and namespace imagePullSecrets.
I'm trying to deploy my application using Helm on an EKS cluster but the deployment is failing and I'm seeing some errors and I'm not sure what's causing it. Can you help me figure out what's wrong?
Fix my Helm deployment.
hint: Giving the exact error message, the platform, and the three most common causes of ImagePullBackOff focuses the diagnosis instantly.

title: Best prompt for Nginx config generation
originalPrompt: You need to generate an Nginx reverse proxy config for two upstream services.
options: Write an Nginx config.
Create an Nginx config file for a reverse proxy. Route /api to upstream api-service:3000 and /static to upstream static-service:8080. Include: gzip, proxy_pass, and connection timeout of 30s.
Help me write an Nginx configuration for routing traffic to different backend services with all the performance and security settings I should include.
hint: Specifying the routes, upstream addresses, and the exact directives needed produces a complete config block on the first try.

title: Multi-region failover design prompt fix
originalPrompt: How should I set up failover for my application so it doesn't go down?
options: Design an active-passive multi-region failover for a stateful payment service: primary us-east-1, standby eu-west-1. RTO ≤5 min, RPO ≤30s. Address: Route 53 health checks, RDS cross-region read replica promotion, and session data sync. Output: architecture decision record.
Tell me how to make my application highly available across multiple regions.
Explain the different strategies for multi-region failover and disaster recovery including active-active and active-passive approaches and their tradeoffs.
hint: Expert prompts require hard SLA numbers (RTO/RPO), specific technology components, and a defined output format to produce actionable architecture decisions.

title: Best monitoring alert rule prompt
originalPrompt: You need to write a Prometheus alert rule for high API error rate.
options: Write a Prometheus alerting rule.

Write a Prometheus alerting rule that fires when the 5xx error rate on /api/v1 exceeds 1% over a 5-minute window. Alert name: HighAPIErrorRate. Severity: critical. Include annotations with a runbook URL placeholder.

Write a comprehensive Prometheus alerting configuration for my API that handles different types of errors and latency and availability with appropriate thresholds and all the metadata fields I should include.
hint: Giving the threshold, window, endpoint scope, and required metadata fields produces a rule that can be dropped straight into an alerts file.

title: Compact chaos engineering scenario prompt
originalPrompt: Ask AI to design a chaos experiment for a microservice.
options: Design a chaos experiment for the checkout service: inject 500ms latency on calls to inventory-service. Hypothesis: circuit breaker triggers within 3 retries. Define: steady state, method (Chaos Monkey), and rollback trigger.

Help me design a chaos engineering experiment for one of my microservices to test its resilience. I want to make sure it handles failures gracefully and I want to understand how to set up the experiment properly with all the right parameters.
How do I do chaos engineering?
hint: Naming the target service, failure mode, hypothesis, tool, and rollback condition gives the AI all the variables needed to produce a runnable experiment plan.

title: Vague Jenkins pipeline prompt fix
originalPrompt: Help me write a Jenkins pipeline for my project.
options: Write a Jenkins declarative pipeline for a Java Maven project. Stages: checkout, build, unit test, Docker build, push to ECR. Fail fast if tests fail. Use environment block for ECR_REPO.
Create a Jenkins pipeline that builds my application and deploys it.
Write a detailed Jenkins pipeline script for me with all the stages and configurations I need.
hint: Listing each stage, the build tool, the registry, and the fail-fast requirement produces a complete pipeline script immediately.

title: Best GitOps migration prompt
originalPrompt: Your team wants to migrate from push-based to GitOps-based deployments using Argo CD.
options: Explain GitOps to me.

Create a migration plan from manual kubectl deployments to GitOps with Argo CD on EKS. We have 12 microservices, 3 environments (dev/staging/prod), and Helm charts. Address: repo structure, Argo CD app-of-apps pattern, secrets management with Sealed Secrets, and a phased rollout. Output: a numbered migration checklist.
What are the best practices for GitOps and how should I structure my repositories and what tools should I use for secrets management?
hint: Describing the current state, the target architecture, the scale, and required components (app-of-apps, secrets) produces a plan the team can execute without further clarification.

title: Quick docker-compose generation request
originalPrompt: Ask AI to write a docker-compose file.
options: Write a docker-compose.yml for a Node.js app + PostgreSQL 15. Mount a named volume for postgres data. Expose app on port 3000. Use .env for DB credentials.
Please write me a docker-compose file for my application. I need a Node.js service and a database service that work together and I want to make sure volumes are set up correctly.
Write docker-compose.
hint: Naming the services, versions, port, volume type, and credential method gives the AI a fully specified target without unnecessary explanation.

title: Cost optimisation prompt for cloud infra
originalPrompt: How can I reduce my AWS costs? I'm spending too much money.
options: Analyse AWS cost optimisation for our stack: EC2 (t3.large, always on), RDS (db.r5.2xlarge, 40% CPU avg), and S3 (2TB, infrequent access). Suggest top 3 savings actions with estimated monthly saving for each.
What are the best ways to reduce AWS costs?
I want to reduce our AWS spending. Can you tell me everything about AWS cost optimisation strategies and reserved instances and savings plans and rightsizing and all the different options?
hint: Providing the specific services, instance types, and utilisation data enables the AI to give dollar-quantified recommendations rather than generic tips.

title: Best zero-downtime deployment prompt
originalPrompt: You need to implement zero-downtime deployment for a stateful microservice.
options: How do I deploy without downtime?

Design a zero-downtime blue-green deployment for a stateful session-based Node.js service on Kubernetes. Address: session affinity during cutover, database migration compatibility, and health check grace period. Return a deployment sequence.
Explain the different deployment strategies like blue-green, canary, and rolling deployments and which one I should use for a stateful application.
hint: Naming the deployment strategy, the statefulness challenge, and the three specific concerns (sessions, DB migrations, health checks) produces a targeted solution.

title: Test plan generation prompt fix
originalPrompt: Write a test plan for my feature. I need to test it properly.
options: Write a QA test plan for a user password reset feature. Cover: functional tests (valid/invalid token, expired token), edge cases, and regression scope. Output as a table with test ID, scenario, steps, and expected result.
Help me create a test plan for the new feature I'm building.
Write a comprehensive and detailed test plan document for my password reset functionality including all possible test scenarios and edge cases and performance tests and security tests.
hint: Naming the feature, the test categories, and a structured table format produces a test plan that can be handed directly to QA engineers.

title: Best Cypress E2E test prompt
originalPrompt: You need to write a Cypress end-to-end test for a login flow.
options: Write a Cypress test for login.

Write a Cypress E2E test for the login flow: valid credentials succeed and redirect to /dashboard, invalid credentials show 'Invalid password' error message. Use cy.intercept to stub the /api/auth endpoint.
Help me write some automated end-to-end tests for my login page that test different scenarios and make sure everything works correctly.
hint: Listing the exact scenarios, expected outcomes, and the specific Cypress API to use produces tests that pass without revision.

title: Bug report generation prompt fix
originalPrompt: I found a bug. Can you help me write up a bug report?
options: Write a Jira bug report for this issue: clicking 'Apply coupon' on checkout with an expired coupon shows no error and removes the cart. Environment: Chrome 124, staging. Steps to reproduce: [paste]. Severity: P1. Include: actual vs expected behaviour.
Help me write a bug report for an issue I found in the checkout flow.
Write a detailed bug report for me for the bug I discovered in our application.
hint: Including the bug location, specific behaviour, environment, severity, and actual vs expected sections maps directly to a standard bug tracking template.

title: Compact test data generation prompt
originalPrompt: Ask AI to generate test data for a user registration form.
options: Generate 5 rows of test data for a user registration form: name, email, password, DOB. Include 1 valid row, 1 invalid email, 1 underage (DOB <18), 1 duplicate email, 1 missing required field.

Can you generate some test data for me for a user registration form? I need different types of data including valid data and invalid data and edge cases so that I can test my form validation properly.
Generate test data.
hint: Specifying the exact number of rows and the specific case each row must represent produces ready-to-use test fixtures.

title: Best performance testing prompt
originalPrompt: You need to load test a checkout API endpoint before a product launch.
options: Help me do load testing.

Write a k6 load test script for POST /api/checkout. Ramp to 500 virtual users over 2 minutes, sustain for 5 minutes. Pass criteria: p95 latency <500ms, error rate <1%. Include a think time of 1s between requests.
Tell me about load testing strategies and different tools like JMeter and k6 and Gatling and when to use each one and what metrics I should be measuring.
hint: Naming the tool, the endpoint, ramp profile, sustain duration, and pass/fail thresholds produces a test script you can run without modification.

title: Test automation strategy prompt fix
originalPrompt: We need a better testing strategy. Can you help us improve our tests?
options: Design a test automation strategy for a React/Node.js e-commerce platform: 200k lines, 15% unit test coverage, 0 E2E tests, 2-hour manual regression cycle. Target: 80% coverage, CI under 10 min. Recommend the testing pyramid split, tooling (Jest/Cypress/Playwright), and a 3-sprint implementation roadmap.
What testing strategies should we use for our application to improve quality?
Help us improve our test coverage and testing approach for our web application with a focus on automation and CI/CD integration.
hint: Quantifying the current state (coverage %, regression time) and the targets enables the AI to produce a gap-based roadmap rather than generic testing advice.

title: Compact API test assertion prompt
originalPrompt: Ask AI to write assertions for an API response.
options: Write Jest assertions for this API response: status 200, body.user.email matches input, body.token is a non-empty string, response time <300ms. Use expect().toEqual and jest-extended matchers.

I need help writing test assertions for the response from my API endpoint. I want to check various things about the response including the status code and the response body structure and the data values and also the response time.
Write API test assertions.
hint: Enumerating the exact assertions and the specific matcher library lets the AI write them directly without needing to infer what to check.

title: Best exploratory testing notes prompt
originalPrompt: You are creating a prompt to document exploratory testing findings.
options: Write up my testing notes.
Convert these raw exploratory testing notes into a structured bug report and testing charter summary. For each issue found, output: title, severity, steps, and actual vs expected. Notes: [paste].
Help me document what I found during exploratory testing of the new feature so the team can understand the issues and what needs to be fixed.
hint: Specifying the two output artefacts and the per-issue structure transforms raw notes into a report that requires no editing.

title: Contract testing prompt improvement
originalPrompt: Help me set up contract testing for my microservices.
options: Generate a Pact consumer contract test in JavaScript for the order-service consumer calling the payment-service provider's POST /payments endpoint. Mock the success (200) and insufficient-funds (402) responses. Include provider verification setup.
What is contract testing and how do I implement it for my microservices architecture?
Help me write contract tests for my services.
hint: Naming the framework, consumer, provider, endpoint, and both response scenarios produces a working Pact test without needing follow-up questions.

title: Compact mutation testing analysis prompt
originalPrompt: Ask AI to analyse mutation testing results.
options: Analyse this Stryker mutation testing report. Mutation score: 34%. Surviving mutants in payment validation: [paste]. Identify top 3 test gaps and suggest one new test case per gap.

I have some mutation testing results from running Stryker on my codebase and the mutation score is quite low. Can you help me understand what the results mean and what I should do to improve my test coverage based on what the mutation testing found?
What do mutation testing results mean?
hint: Giving the tool name, the score, the surviving mutants, and asking for top-3 gaps with one test case each keeps the response actionable and within the token budget.

title: Vague ETL pipeline prompt improvement
originalPrompt: Help me build a data pipeline that processes some data.
options: Design a Python ETL pipeline to ingest daily CSV files from S3, transform (deduplicate, parse timestamps to UTC, drop nulls in user_id), and load to a Redshift staging table. Use pandas for transformation.
Can you help me build a pipeline that takes some data and processes it and loads it somewhere?
Write a comprehensive data pipeline for me that handles ingestion, transformation, and loading with all the best practices and error handling.
hint: Specifying source, destination, the three transformations, and the library gives the AI enough to write runnable code immediately.

title: Best SQL optimisation prompt
originalPrompt: A Redshift query joining three large tables is taking 8 minutes to run.
options: Make my SQL query faster.

Optimise this Redshift SQL query that takes 8 minutes. It joins orders (500M rows), customers (10M rows), and products (1M rows). EXPLAIN output: [paste]. Focus on: distribution keys, sort keys, and join order. Query: [paste].
Tell me about SQL query optimisation techniques for Redshift and how I can improve my queries that involve large table joins.
hint: Providing row counts, the EXPLAIN output, and the query itself along with Redshift-specific knobs (dist/sort keys) enables a targeted optimisation plan.

title: Data quality check prompt fix
originalPrompt: I need to check data quality. Can you help?
options: Write Great Expectations test suite for a transactions DataFrame. Check: no nulls in transaction_id or amount, amount > 0, transaction_date is a valid ISO 8601 timestamp, and user_id matches UUID format. Return as Python code.
Help me add data quality checks to my data pipeline to ensure the data is correct.
Write a very thorough data quality validation framework for my data pipeline that checks everything that could possibly be wrong with the data.
hint: Naming the validation library, the DataFrame, and each specific rule produces production-ready Great Expectations code without iteration.

title: Compact data schema design request
originalPrompt: Ask AI to design a database schema for e-commerce orders.
options: Design a PostgreSQL schema for e-commerce orders. Tables: orders, order_items, customers. Include PKs, FKs, and indexes on customer_id and created_at. Return CREATE TABLE statements only.

I need help designing a database schema for an e-commerce platform. I want tables for orders and customers and products and I need to think about the relationships between them and the right indexes to use.
Design a database schema.
hint: Naming the tables, the key constraints, and the indexes and limiting output to CREATE TABLE statements keeps the response lean and usable.

title: Best streaming pipeline architecture prompt
originalPrompt: You need to design a real-time data streaming pipeline for clickstream events.
options: How do I process streaming data?

Design a real-time clickstream pipeline: 100k events/sec, Kafka ingestion, Flink processing (sessionisation window: 30 min inactivity), sink to ClickHouse. Address: event ordering guarantees, late-arriving data (up to 5 min), and backpressure strategy.
Help me design a streaming data architecture for clickstream events with the right technologies and patterns for real-time processing.
hint: Providing throughput numbers, the full tech stack, window parameters, and the three failure modes (ordering, late data, backpressure) produces a complete architecture.

title: Data lake architecture prompt fix

Define: 3 change types (standard/normal/emergency) with approval workflows, a change advisory board (CAB) meeting cadence, a change request template (fields: description, risk, rollback plan, test evidence), and a KPI to measure process effectiveness (change success rate). Output: a process flowchart description.
Tell me about ITIL change management best practices and how to implement a change control process in an IT organisation.
hint: Defining the three change types, CAB cadence, template fields, and a success KPI with a flowchart output produces a complete change management framework.

title: Compact smoke test checklist prompt
originalPrompt: Ask AI to create a smoke test checklist for a web application deployment.
options: Create a 6-item smoke test checklist for a web app post-deployment. Cover: homepage loads (200 OK), user login, key API endpoint responds, database connectivity, third-party integrations (Stripe ping), and error page renders. Format: checkbox list with pass condition per item.
Can you create a smoke test checklist I can use after deploying my web application to make sure everything is working correctly?
Write smoke tests.
hint: Fixing the item count and naming each specific check with a pass condition produces a checklist that can be executed in under 5 minutes.

title: Compact access review checklist prompt
originalPrompt: Ask AI to create a quarterly access review checklist.
options: Create a quarterly user access review checklist for a SaaS company. 6 items covering: inactive accounts (no login >90 days), over-privileged roles, service account usage, MFA compliance, shared credentials, and offboarded employee cleanup. Format: checkbox with responsible owner per item.
I need a checklist for our quarterly access review process to make sure we are reviewing all user accounts and permissions across our systems.
Write access review checklist.
hint: Listing the six specific access risks and adding a responsible owner per item makes the checklist assignable and auditable.
